tasks.title: Recover failed April 28 production deploy on GCP
tasks.description: Restore Cloudflare tunnel, production CMS/integrations data, Odoo health, and rebuild the live web app from the current VM source.
evidence: Recovered production Cloudflare tunnel by restoring the complete .env.prod backup on the GCP VM and bringing up cloudflared first.
Recreated production Postgres/Odoo service state and verified odoo container returned to healthy status.
Restored CMS and integrations snapshots plus managed home/logo/favicon blobs on the VM and in galante_cms_settings so social links and cover assets survived restarts.
Hardened production web rebuilds by adding NODE_OPTIONS heap sizing in Dockerfile and excluding _codex_stage from .dockerignore.
Rebuilt the production web container from current VM source and verified the shop sidebar filters layout is live again.
Validated public home, /shop, admin login, and Odoo with HTTP checks plus Selenium functional smoke on April 28, 2026.

tasks.title: Repair post-recovery shop regressions and revalidate production
tasks.description: Fix production shop interaction and PDP image issues, then run Selenium verification across the live environment.
evidence: Moved shop sorting, category filters, and pagination to direct URL navigation so production filtering works reliably again.
Aligned PDP product gallery image delivery with the direct product image route to eliminate the failing _next/image optimization path.
Disabled browser extensions inside the cloned Selenium runtime profile to prevent third-party content scripts from contaminating production verification.
Passed Selenium production verification on April 28, 2026 across home, shop, contact, bridal, collections, PDP, and /api/health with zero failed checks.

tasks.title: Restore Google OAuth owner connection and production Calendar routing
tasks.description: Reproduce the failing Google OAuth reconnection flow with the operational Chrome profile, fix the callback/token exchange path, restore the production calendar ID, and verify live Calendar creation on the CEO calendar.
evidence: Reproduced the exact Google OAuth policy error with Selenium using the persistent cloned Chrome Profile 9 runtime.
Fixed the admin OAuth callback so token exchange always uses the live request-derived redirect URI instead of a percent-encoded cookie value.
Completed production owner OAuth reconnection and persisted a fresh encrypted refresh token in data/integrations.json.
Restored production appointments.googleCalendarId to ceo@galantesjewelry.com from the admin panel.
Adjusted Calendar auth selection to prefer the configured service account for Calendar operations while keeping owner OAuth available for Gmail API mail delivery.
Validated with Selenium that /api/admin/verify-calendar returned success and produced an event whose decoded eid targets ceo@galantesjewelry.com.

tasks.title: Harden customer account redirects

Added tests/unit/app/account/account-pages.test.ts to verify the three redirect paths.
Rebuilt production web and verified the live /account redirects still return HTTP 307.

Hardened app/api/shipping/rates/route.ts and app/api/checkout/stripe/route.ts with a fast fallback when CMS settings are slow to hydrate from Odoo.
Validated with vitest, TypeScript, next build, and Selenium smoke on the local checkout flow using Profile 9.

tasks.title: Harden production web security and validate account UI

Added focused unit coverage for product-image traversal rejection and auth route rate limiting; full npm run test passed with 29 files and 93 tests.

Verified production /api/health returns 200 with security headers, /api/products/image?id=../../package returns 400, and https://odoo.galantesjewelry.com/web/login still returns Odoo 200 OK.
Ran Selenium Profile 9 production checks: product catalog smoke passed and account navbar smoke passed for /account/orders, /account/invoices, and /account/settings.
Measured post-deploy public performance: home TTFB 0.215s total 0.236s; shop TTFB 0.255s total 0.268s; main containers healthy and root disk 73% used.

tasks.title: Improve checkout fulfillment for signed-in customers
tasks.description: Autofill checkout from authenticated customer profile data, let customers choose saved Odoo addresses, add a first-class boutique pickup path, and validate/deploy the flow.
evidence: Added GET /api/account/profile so checkout can safely preload the authenticated customer's name, email, phone, and default Odoo profile address without exposing secrets.

Updated app/checkout/page.tsx with a user-friendly fulfillment step: Ship insured vs Boutique pickup, contact autocompletion, saved address cards, manual address fallback, and pickup flow that skips shipping address inputs.
Updated components/checkout/ShippingSelector.tsx to support excluding pickup from carrier rates when pickup is handled as its own top-level fulfillment option.
Updated app/api/checkout/stripe/route.ts so pickup orders are accepted without shipping address fields while delivery orders still require a valid address and server-side insured rate validation.

Replaced the checkout summary Next Image usage for /api/products/image?id=... thumbnails with a deliberately scoped img fallback because Next 16 localPatterns blocks broad local query-string optimization.
Added tests/playwright/checkout-fulfillment.spec.ts covering signed-in checkout prefill, saved address selection, shipping rate selection, and pickup without address fields.
Extended tests/unit/app/api/checkout-stripe-route.test.ts to cover boutique pickup without a shipping address.
Validated locally with checkout Stripe unit tests, TypeScript, lint, Next production build, and Playwright against http://127.0.0.1:3002.
Created production backup /home/yoeli/deploy-backups/checkout-fulfillment-20260503-005404.tgz, deployed the selected web files, rebuilt galantes_web, and restarted only the web service.
Validated production health and ran Playwright checkout fulfillment against https://galantesjewelry.com with API interception so no payment/order was created.

Follow-up hardening on May 3, 2026: Use a new address now clears prior saved-address fields, /api/checkout/stripe rejects pickup rates unless deliveryMethod is explicitly pickup, and GET /api/account/profile has unit coverage for authenticated and unauthenticated responses.

Validated the follow-up with 6 focused Vitest tests, TypeScript, lint, local Playwright, Next production build, production backup /home/yoeli/deploy-backups/checkout-edge-hardening-20260503-014217.tgz, web-only deploy, public health check, and production Playwright with API interception.

tasks.title: Integrate FedEx live rates into shipping provider selection

evidence: Added lib/shipping/fedex.ts with FedEx OAuth token caching, rate quote retrieval, response normalization, and an estimated fallback when credentials are missing or the API fails.
Updated lib/shipping/engine.ts to surface pickup, USPS, UPS, and FedEx rates together and sort selectable options by price.
Added .env.example shipping provider variables for FEDEX_BASE_URL, FEDEX_CLIENT_ID, FEDEX_CLIENT_SECRET, and FEDEX_ACCOUNT_NUMBER.
Added tests/unit/lib/shipping/fedex.test.ts and tests/unit/lib/shipping/engine.test.ts to cover live FedEx normalization, fallback behavior, and carrier ordering.
Validated the new shipping flow with 7 Vitest assertions, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run lint, and npm run build.

tasks.title: Fix location-based tax calculation and cart thumbnails
tasks.description: Calculate tax from the shipping or pickup destination, charge Stripe with the final Odoo order total, and fall back to the product image API when cart thumbnails are missing image_url data.
evidence: Added a shared lib/tax.ts helper that computes tax from the destination address instead of the payment method.
Updated the cart summary API, checkout page, and checkout Stripe route to use the shared tax logic and the store pickup address as a pickup fallback.
Changed the cart and checkout thumbnail rendering to fall back to /api/products/image?id=... when image_url is missing.
Verified the new logic with focused Vitest coverage for tax math and checkout totals, plus a Playwright cart flow that confirms the fallback thumbnail and rendered totals.

tasks.title: Consolidate production QA entrypoints and Selenium runtime discipline
tasks.description: Create a single production QA suite with safe defaults, guard mutating production checks behind explicit flags, and route legacy Selenium entrypoints back through the shared profile-runtime path.
evidence: Added tests/e2e/production_suite.py as the main production QA runner with artifact reporting, safe default coverage, and explicit flags for account-portal, checkout-payment, and real appointment send flows.
Added npm scripts test:e2e:production and test:e2e:production:full so operators have a single documented entrypoint for production QA.
Updated tests/e2e/README.md to document the new production suite, its guarded live-mutation behavior, and the legacy-entrypoint guidance.

Replaced legacy direct Selenium entrypoints in tests/e2e/verify_prod_live.py, tests/e2e/prod_full_verification.py, tests/e2e/qa_functional_validation.py, and tests/e2e/qa_diag.py with shared-runtime wrappers so they no longer bypass the required profile strategy.

tasks.title: Raise Odoo product gallery upload resolution for phone photos
tasks.description: Increase the custom Odoo gallery image cap so high-resolution phone photos can be uploaded to product records in production without the previous aggressive downscale limit.
evidence: Updated odoo/addons/galantes_jewelry/models/product_gallery.py so galantes.product.gallery.image now allows max_width=4096 and max_height=4096 instead of 1024.
Bumped the addon manifest version in odoo/addons/galantes_jewelry/__manifest__.py to 19.0.1.0.1 for traceability.
Synced the updated addon files directly to /home/yoeli/galantesjewelry on galantes-prod-vm and restarted only the galantes_odoo service.
Validated the mounted production addon code reflects the 4096x4096 limits, the Odoo container returned to healthy status, and https://odoo.galantesjewelry.com/web/login still responded with HTTP 200.

tasks.title: Reset production catalog for clean inventory replacement
tasks.description: Back up production, remove the current customer-facing Odoo products while preserving historical references, prevent seeded demo products from being reintroduced on the next addon upgrade, and clear the storefront cache so the public shop renders empty.
evidence: Created a full production database backup at /home/yoeli/deploy-backups/inventory-reset-20260508_135719.galantes_prod.dump before any destructive catalog change.
Updated odoo/addons/galantes_jewelry/data/product_data.xml from noupdate=0 to noupdate=1 and synced it to the GCP VM so future addon upgrades do not automatically reseed the demo catalog.

Removed all 35 non-service, non-placeholder product templates in galantes_prod through the Odoo ORM with an explicit commit, leaving only the Standard delivery service, the internal Galantes Secure Shipping service, and the archival deleted-product placeholder.

Forced all remaining service templates off the storefront, restarted galantes_web to clear in-memory product fallback cache, and verified the public https://shop.galantesjewelry.com/shop page returned HTTP 200 with the empty-state UI instead of old product entries.

tasks.title: Validate production invoice and refund flow against Odoo and Stripe
tasks.description: Repair the production Odoo accounting baseline enough to validate paid checkout finalization, automatic invoice creation, idempotent payment sync, and a real Stripe refund/credit-note flow using a temporary QA order.
evidence: Added odoo/addons/galantes_jewelry/models/__init__.py import coverage for sale_order, synced the file to the GCP VM, restarted galantes_odoo, and confirmed sale.order.action_galantes_finalize_paid_checkout is now registered in production.

Inspected the production accounting baseline and found My Company had zero accounts, zero journals, and zero payment method lines; installed l10n_us + l10n_us_account, loaded the us chart template directly on galantes_prod, and confirmed 106 accounts plus 7 journals were created.
Created a temporary out-of-stock QA product, partner, and sale order in production Odoo, then created a real Stripe test-mode PaymentIntent for the exact order total.

Ran sale.order.action_galantes_finalize_paid_checkout on production order S00010 and verified it created exactly one posted invoice INV/2026/00001, registered one inbound bank payment, and stayed idempotent on a second call with the same Stripe payload.

Executed a full Stripe refund re_3TUpEb9lJ2VzyN5R1HEgh1o7, created and posted the Odoo credit note RINV/2026/00001, registered the outbound refund payment in the Bank journal, and confirmed a second Stripe refund attempt is blocked with charge_already_refunded.

Validated the mismatch guard by proving action_galantes_finalize_paid_checkout raises UserError when the Stripe amount does not match the Odoo invoice total, then archived the temporary QA product so the storefront catalog stays clean.

tasks.title: Promote Odoo addon and trigger live testing

Identified and resolved production issue where Postgres credentials were disconnected from Odoo.
Executed localized JSON-RPC script on production to verify Model galante.appointment exists.
Updated the Odoo admin internal password natively to match Next.js .env.prod config.
Confirmed Next.js is safely blocked behind Google Calendar configuration without throwing backend crash.
